title: Databricks Makes Spark Easier
period: 2013–today: from open-source engine to managed platform
summary: Spark's own creators founded Databricks to take away the hard part: managing infrastructure. Clusters with a click, serverless compute, collaborative notebooks, and scheduled jobs — all in the cloud.

context.headline: The engine, wrapped in an easy platform
context.description: Apache Spark was powerful but hard to operate. Databricks, founded by Spark's creators, wraps the engine in a managed cloud platform: spin up compute with a click (or go serverless), work in collaborative notebooks, and schedule production jobs — without wrangling servers. On Microsoft's cloud, it's a first-party service called Azure Databricks.
context.when: From founding the company to today's serverless, collaborative platform
